input_schema.hcom_message_example: hcom send --name <sender> "@research-pipeline-claude-6 validate: <input_id> 02_extract; path=outputs/<input_id>/02_extract/options/{A,B,C}/v1.json; schema=schemas/02_extract.json; report verdict to @research-pipeline-claude-1"

process.description: Run validate_artifact(input_id, stage, version, ext, llm_judge_score=None): schema check (jsonschema.Draft7Validator vs schemas/<stage>.json; TXT stages get status: skip) + completeness check (all required fields present, non-null, non-'', non-{}).
If schema+completeness = fail, do NOT bother with LLM-judge — surface the error.
Read the artifact. Form a 0.0-1.0 quality score grounded in the per-stage quality bar (see skills/<stage>/SKILL.md self-check sections).
Re-call validate_artifact(input_id, stage, version, ext, llm_judge_score=score) to record the score.
Call tools.manifest.record_attempt(input_id, stage, version, status, score, feedback=...).
Reply to @orch with verdict: <input_id> <stage> + pass|fail score=N feedback=...
For multi-option (02_extract): for each option A/B/C, repeat steps 1-7 to record a score per option. Pick the highest-scoring option.

For multi-option: call tools.artifact_io.pick_winner(input_id, '02_extract', option_scores, ext='json') — copies the winner's v1.json to the stage root, writes v1.meta.json with picked_option + picked_score.
Re-validate the stage-root copy with the winner's LLM-judge score.
Call record_attempt(input_id, '02_extract', 1, status, score, options=3, picked=<letter>, feedback=...).

examples.lifted_from: real example: score 0.85 -> pass, score 0.5 -> fail

self_check.check: Single-option flow: called validate_artifact twice (None then score)
Multi-option flow: scored each option separately; called pick_winner with the correct option_scores dict
Stage-root copy: for 02_extract, v1.json + v1.meta.json exist with picked_option + picked_score
Meta validation block filled: status, validator (='critic'), validated_at, score, feedback, checks
Manifest recorded: record_attempt called with correct status, score, options (multi-option), picked (multi-option)
Reply to @orch includes pass|fail, score=, feedback= (for retries)
On multi-option, did NOT overrule the highest-scoring option
Did NOT edit any v1.json content (only meta + stage-root copy via pick_winner)
Did not self-validate (N/A here, but principle holds)
Threshold respected: pass iff score >= pipeline.json -> critic.llm_judge_threshold (default 0.7)
Defaulted to pass when in doubt (per this spec's Process step 5)
Tiebreak documented (per :65): if scores tied, picked higher schema+completeness; documented in feedback

check_refs: N/A — this IS the validation. The critic is the only validator (per AGENTS.md invariant #3)

common_failures.fix: BLOCK; orchestrator halts; fix by ensuring schemas/<stage>.json exists
Surface verbatim; orchestrator halts; fix by re-writing the artifact
Tiebreak on schema+completeness; document in feedback
Feedback says 'exhausted retries; orchestrator should halt'

failure_modes.name: Schema file missing for this stage
failure_modes.handling: BLOCK with feedback 'schema missing: <path>'. Orchestrator halts.

failure_modes.name: Artifact is not valid JSON
failure_modes.handling: Schema check fails with the parse error. Surface it in feedback.

failure_modes.name: LLM judge returns a tie
failure_modes.handling: Pick the option that ranks higher on schema + completeness. Document the tiebreak in feedback.

failure_modes.name: All 3 retries fail
failure_modes.handling: Write feedback that explicitly says 'exhausted retries; orchestrator should halt'. Record each attempt in manifest.

refactor_delta.current_state: 3-checks table + multi-option process in this spec's Process sections 'B. Multi-option stages' + 'C. The 3 checks table'. The LLM-judge is the critic's own judgment — no shared rubric, so scores may vary across runs.
refactor_delta.target_state: An eval rubric (JSON or prompt) makes the LLM-judge reproducible.
concrete_steps: Keep the 3-checks table + multi-option process in this spec (already moved).
Author tools/eval_rubric.json with per-stage dimensions + weight.
Add scripts/smoke_llm_judge.py that runs the same input 5 times and asserts score variance < 0.1.
